mission: Create the NHS login credentials, redirect-URI, scopes, environment-profile, and client-registration execution pack in two explicit parts: a high-fidelity local mock NHS login service plus admin console now, and a gated real-provider credential-capture strategy later.

official_guidance.title: What is NHS login?
official_guidance.summary: NHS login is an OpenID Connect-based identity rail for health and care services. It is not a one-off GP-credential retrieval tool.
grounding: NHS login is how people prove who they are online for health and care websites or apps.
Partners choose the required verification and authentication combination for access.
The service must not be used as a one-off linkage-key retrieval path.

official_guidance.title: How NHS login works
official_guidance.summary: NHS login authenticates and verifies the user, but partner services own session management, logout, age controls, and post-auth product authorization.
grounding: Consent denial must return to the partner with an explicit code and bounded handling.
Session management and logout remain partner responsibilities.
PDS checks and GP credentials refresh happen over repeated use; NHS login does not replace PDS.

official_guidance.title: How do I integrate to the sandpit?
official_guidance.summary: Sandpit registration requires a friendly name, redirect URI, public key, and scope list. Sandpit is for proof of concept and login-flow rehearsal.
grounding: Partners submit a sandpit environment request form.
Required setup data is the friendly name, redirect URI, public key, and scopes.
Partners should complete login flow testing across token and userinfo before attempting registration journeys.

official_guidance.title: Compare NHS login environments
official_guidance.summary: Sandpit has no formal requirements, integration requires sandpit completion plus ODS and DSPT posture, and live needs readiness activity plus a signed agreement.
grounding: Sandpit allows proof-of-concept login rehearsal but not real ID or load testing.
Integration is where technical conformance and production-like testing occur.
Live provides only bounded smoke-test coverage, not general experimentation.

official_guidance.title: Test data
official_guidance.summary: Sandpit provides dummy accounts and a static OTP; integration supports PDS-backed end-to-end test data and optional IM1 linkage details; live can provide a smoke-test user only.
grounding: Sandpit accounts use dummy details and a static OTP for rehearsals.
Integration provides test accounts for full end-to-end journeys and can support IM1 linkage details.
Supplier test-data requests have required fields and explicit GP IM1 optional sections.

official_guidance.title: Multiple redirect URIs
official_guidance.summary: NHS login supports up to 10 redirect URIs and recommends using opaque state-based fan-out when more destinations are needed.
grounding: The state parameter should carry the partner-side routing identifier.
The callback returns state as-is to the partner.
Callback sprawl beyond 10 URIs must be handled by partner-side fan-out.

official_guidance.title: Technical Conformance
official_guidance.summary: Integration environment testing covers production-like setup and technical conformance; IM1 and non-IM1 suppliers have distinct test-data expectations.
grounding: IM1 suppliers create local GP-system records and share linkage details with NHS login for integration testing.
Non-IM1 suppliers can still test online identity verification and GP Online journeys in integration.
Integration is the correct place for production-like conformance evidence, not sandpit.

official_guidance.title: Using NHS login to create or retrieve GP credentials
official_guidance.summary: The `gp_integration_credentials` scope returns linkage key, ODS code, and account ID for IM1-enabled relying parties only, and only as part of NHS login authentication.
grounding: The feature must be used as part of NHS login authentication, not as a standalone retrieval flow.
IM1-enabled production posture is required before the scope is allowed in live environments.
Relying parties must avoid endless retry loops when GP credentials cannot be refreshed.

official_guidance.title: Scopes and claims
official_guidance.summary: Scopes are not automatically available: they must be requested and approved. `openid` is mandatory, `profile` and `basic_demographics` are mutually exclusive, and `gp_integration_credentials` is high-assurance and IM1-gated.
grounding: Claims are retrieved through the userinfo endpoint after approval.
The `openid` scope is mandatory for all partners.
`gp_integration_credentials` is available only to IM1-enabled partners or approved third-party pairings.

official_guidance.title: Introduction to Vectors of Trust
official_guidance.summary: The `vtr` claim requests acceptable verification and authentication combinations. Returned `vot` and `vtm` claims bind the achieved trust level to the token.
grounding: VoT selection must be included during OIDC initialisation.
The default NHS login vector set assumes high verification unless a partner specifies otherwise.
Services with mixed-sensitivity features should step up from medium to high only when needed.

environment_profiles.label: Local mock
environment_profiles.test_data_posture: Synthetic aliases, static OTP, deterministic callback-race and stale-code fault injection.
environment_profiles.technical_conformance_posture: Simulator only; validates contract seams, never claims NHS conformance.
environment_profiles.gp_integration_posture: Disabled by default; can simulate `gp_integration_credentials` only when IM1 flag is enabled on the client.
environment_profiles.official_alignment: Local-only rehearsal substrate that mirrors the control seams later exercised in sandpit and integration.

environment_profiles.label: Sandpit-like
environment_profiles.test_data_posture: Prepared dummy accounts plus static OTP rehearsal; no real ID or full PDS-backed registration.
environment_profiles.technical_conformance_posture: No technical conformance claims; mirrors sandpit restrictions and form requirements only.
environment_profiles.gp_integration_posture: IM1 registration flow stays disabled, matching sandpit restrictions on GP Online testing.
environment_profiles.official_alignment: Matches sandpit request fields and login-flow rehearsal posture from current guidance.

environment_profiles.label: Integration-like
environment_profiles.test_data_posture: Synthetic PDS-matched and IM1-ready aliases mirror integration test-pack structure.
environment_profiles.technical_conformance_posture: Exercises conformance-era callback, userinfo, and IM1 refresh rules without touching a real portal.
environment_profiles.gp_integration_posture: Available only when client and test user both carry IM1 enablement.
environment_profiles.official_alignment: Mirrors the integration environment’s conformance and test-data expectations.

environment_profiles.label: Actual sandpit later
environment_profiles.test_data_posture: Provider-issued sandpit client plus supplied test pack after request approval.
environment_profiles.technical_conformance_posture: No conformance sign-off here; sandpit remains a proof-of-concept environment.
environment_profiles.gp_integration_posture: Do not assume GP Online or IM1 testing is available; keep it disabled until integration.
environment_profiles.official_alignment: Requires friendly name, redirect URI, public key, and scope list on the sandpit request form.

environment_profiles.label: Actual integration later
environment_profiles.test_data_posture: Provider integration pack plus optional IM1 linkage details after approval.
environment_profiles.technical_conformance_posture: Formal technical conformance, SCAL evidence, and environment review belong here.
environment_profiles.gp_integration_posture: IM1 pathways are allowed only with approved pairings and explicit high-assurance scope approval.
environment_profiles.official_alignment: Requires completed sandpit work, ODS code, DSPT posture, and conformance testing.

environment_profiles.label: Actual production later
environment_profiles.test_data_posture: Smoke-test user only, no exploratory testing, and no load testing.
environment_profiles.technical_conformance_posture: Permitted only after readiness activity, agreement, and provider approval.
environment_profiles.gp_integration_posture: Production `gp_integration_credentials` remains blocked until live IM1 enablement is approved.
environment_profiles.official_alignment: Live environment requires completed readiness activity and signed agreement.

vot_profiles.label: Low : Basic
vot_profiles.suitable_for: Low-trust sign-in upgrades, public-to-authenticated transitions, and controlled claim-precheck flows.

vot_profiles.label: Medium : Strong
vot_profiles.suitable_for: Messages, callback continuity, and basic self-service features that do not grant sensitive-record mutation.

vot_profiles.label: High : Strong
vot_profiles.suitable_for: Sensitive records, appointments management, and any path that needs high verification before local capability can widen.

vot_profiles.label: Medium plus step-up to high
vot_profiles.suitable_for: Mixed-sensitivity portals where health records or IM1 pairing need a later step-up journey instead of universal high friction.

scope_bundles.mutual_exclusion_note: Does not request `profile`; keeps the first sign-in uplift narrow.
scope_bundles.notes: For sign-in uplift from intake or settings-link recovery where local capability still decides whether the shell becomes read-only or claim-pending.
Default bundle for signed-in home, requests, appointments, messages, and record access.

scope_bundles.mutual_exclusion_note: Use only where the later environment and business case permit `profile_extended`.
scope_bundles.notes: Kept available for integration-like rehearsal but not widened by default in sandpit-like or public intake flows.
Disabled unless the client is IM1-enabled and the route policy explicitly allows GP credential pairing as part of sign-in.

route_bindings.notes: Auth success may only upgrade the public intake shell into an authenticated or claim-pending posture; it never grants clinical writability by itself.
Keeps grant-scoped recovery narrow and bound to the current route intent and subject lineage.
Home remains projection-only after auth until local capability law admits wider actions.
Reply, claim, and recovery actions remain route-bound after local session establishment.
Booking truth, waitlist, and manage posture require high verification before local capability can widen.
Sensitive-record access needs high verification, but the local shell still controls artifact visibility and actionability.
Messaging reply and callback repair stay bound to reachability and current thread tuples after auth.
Settings-link simulations never imply broader record access; they only prove safe return handling.
Explicitly gated behind IM1 enablement and never available as a one-off retrieval shortcut.
Future-only route binding kept in the pack so Phase 7 cannot silently widen current callback posture.

mock_clients.label: Service patient portal
mock_clients.notes: Primary client for standard signed-in patient work.

mock_clients.label: Service recovery bridge
mock_clients.notes: Narrow client for recovery, upgrade, and safe return handling.

mock_clients.label: Service IM1 pairing
mock_clients.notes: Explicit IM1-only client. Disabled in sandpit-like environments and blocked in live until IM1 approval exists.

mock_clients.label: Service embedded future
mock_clients.notes: Deferred channel placeholder only; not part of the current baseline.

auth_scenarios.label: Authorize to token to userinfo happy path

live_gates.label: Phase 0 external-readiness gate
live_gates.summary: seq_020 still reports `withheld` for Phase 0 entry; no real provider mutation may proceed while the external-readiness chain remains withheld.

live_gates.label: NHS login partner approval
live_gates.summary: Real client IDs, redirect registration, and production credentials stay placeholder-only until the NHS login partner onboarding path and named approver identity are complete.

live_gates.label: Redirect and environment review
live_gates.summary: The redirect matrix is generated and route-family bound, but a later human review must approve any real sandpit or live redirect registration.

live_gates.label: Identity and session parity proof
live_gates.summary: This pack binds redirect URIs, VoT profiles, scopes, and callback outcomes to the blueprint’s route-intent and local-session law.

live_gates.label: Environment target present
live_gates.summary: Real runs must specify sandpit, integration, or production as a named target. The pack intentionally leaves the field blank.

live_gates.label: Live mutation flag enabled
live_gates.summary: All generated browser automation defaults to dry-run and refuses live submission until ALLOW_REAL_PROVIDER_MUTATION=true.

live_gates.label: IM1 and SCAL approval
live_gates.summary: Any real `gp_integration_credentials` use remains blocked until IM1, SCAL, and approved third-party pairing evidence are current.

live_gates.label: Technical conformance and regression pack
live_gates.summary: The simulator covers callback, userinfo, stale-code, and redirect rules now, but official technical conformance still belongs to the actual integration environment.

live_gates.label: Phase 0 external-readiness gate
live_gates.summary: seq_020 still reports `withheld` for Phase 0 entry; no real provider mutation may proceed while the external-readiness chain remains withheld.

live_gates.label: NHS login partner approval
live_gates.summary: Real client IDs, redirect registration, and production credentials stay placeholder-only until the NHS login partner onboarding path and named approver identity are complete.

live_gates.label: Redirect and environment review
live_gates.summary: The redirect matrix is generated and route-family bound, but a later human review must approve any real sandpit or live redirect registration.

live_gates.label: Identity and session parity proof
live_gates.summary: This pack binds redirect URIs, VoT profiles, scopes, and callback outcomes to the blueprint’s route-intent and local-session law.

live_gates.label: Environment target present
live_gates.summary: Real runs must specify sandpit, integration, or production as a named target. The pack intentionally leaves the field blank.

live_gates.label: Live mutation flag enabled
live_gates.summary: All generated browser automation defaults to dry-run and refuses live submission until ALLOW_REAL_PROVIDER_MUTATION=true.

live_gates.label: IM1 and SCAL approval
live_gates.summary: Any real `gp_integration_credentials` use remains blocked until IM1, SCAL, and approved third-party pairing evidence are current.

live_gates.label: Technical conformance and regression pack
live_gates.summary: The simulator covers callback, userinfo, stale-code, and redirect rules now, but official technical conformance still belongs to the actual integration environment.

placeholder_fields.label: Named approver
placeholder_fields.notes: Must identify the operator who may later pause-and-confirm a real submission run.

placeholder_fields.label: Environment target
placeholder_fields.notes: Must be one of sandpit, integration, or production.

placeholder_fields.label: Sandpit client ID
placeholder_fields.notes: Capture to quarantine first, then move to metadata registry after review.

placeholder_fields.label: Integration client ID
placeholder_fields.notes: Becomes active only after product demo and environment approval.

placeholder_fields.label: Production client ID
placeholder_fields.notes: Must never be committed to the repository or screenshots.

placeholder_fields.label: Public-key submission ref
placeholder_fields.notes: The private key remains outside the repo; this field records the approved public-key reference only.

placeholder_fields.label: Redirect review ref
placeholder_fields.notes: Must refer to the generated route-family redirect matrix rather than ad hoc form text.

placeholder_fields.label: Scope approval ref
placeholder_fields.notes: Records the approved scope bundle and VTR for later real onboarding.

placeholder_fields.label: Provider test pack reference
placeholder_fields.notes: Captures the provider-issued test pack reference; not the raw spreadsheet itself.

placeholder_fields.label: IM1 pairing reference
placeholder_fields.notes: Only complete when the IM1-enabled flag, SCAL, and approved third-party posture all exist.

placeholder_fields.label: Allow real provider mutation
placeholder_fields.notes: Must remain false by default and requires a pause-before-submit acknowledgement.

vault_ingest_flow.action: Promote live secret material into preprod_vault or production_vault only after dual review
Reference signing material by HSM key ID only; never expose private-key bytes to browser automation
